introduction:
supertool batches multiple file operations into one Bash call.

Invoke via Bash:
./supertool \
    'read:src/app/Module.py' \
    'read:src/app/Config.py' \
    'grep:class:src/app/:20' \
    'glob:src/app/**/*.py'

Rules:
- One call, many ops. 6-7 per call is normal. Each separate call wastes money.
- Paths are relative to project root.
- Single quotes around each op.
- Use Read tool ONLY for the file you're about to Edit — supertool for everything else.
output-format:
Each operation returns a header followed by its output:

```
--- read:src/app/Module.py ---
(45 lines, 1230 bytes)
     1→import os
     2→import sys
     3→
     4→class Module:

--- grep:class:src/app/:5 ---
(2 results, limit 5)
src/app/Module.py
  4:class Module:
src/app/Config.py
  8:class Config:

--- glob:src/app/**/*.py ---
(3 files)
src/app/Module.py
src/app/Config.py
src/app/Utils.py
```

read-grep.description: Inline filter — matching lines, line nums kept

grep.description: Search (10 results def). CONTEXT=N lines around match

grep-count.description: Count mode — match counts/file, no content

around.description: First match + N lines ctx (def 10). 1-shot grep+read

around_line.description: N lines around LINE (def 10). Target marked →

between.description: Chunk of file. Symbol mode (tree-sitter, multi-lang) or re:START:END pattern mode

blame.description: Git blame N lines around LINE (def 5)
